metrics.definition: Describes the level of fragmentation of streams—the extent to which streams and rivers are fragmented into unconnected parts—present on land directly or indirectly controlled by the organization as of the end of the reporting period. Report for hectares controlled at any point during the reporting period. Choose one: - Very low fragmentation - Low fragmentation - Moderate fragmentation - High fragmentation - Very high fragmentation - No data

metrics.name: Revenue Generated at Directly Supported/Financed Enterprises

metrics.name: Client Individuals: Poor
metrics.definition: Number of unique poor individuals who were clients of the organization during the reporting period.

metrics.name: Number of Renewals
metrics.definition: Number of policies belonging to the organization’s clients that were renewed during the reporting period after the coverage term expired.

metrics.name: Public Water Point Coverage
metrics.definition: Number of individuals within a coverage region (defined by a municipality, district, or designated service area) who have primary water access through a public tap as of the end of the reporting period.

metrics.name: Loss of Marine Habitat
metrics.definition: Total surface area of marine or coastal habitat lost, including both direct destruction and unrestored degradation, as a result of the financed activity, such as coastal infrastructure development, dredging, seabed mining, aquaculture, or other marine and coastal land use changes.

metrics.name: Length of Streams Present
metrics.definition: Length of freshwater streams present during the reporting period on protected land, land under sustainable stewardship, or land under sustainable cultivation.

metrics.name: Value of Voluntary Savings Accounts

metrics.name: Client Households: Historically Marginalized

metrics.name: Client Time Savings

metrics.name: Number of Individual Connections

metrics.name: School Enrollment: Peri-urban
metrics.definition: Number of students residing in peri-urban areas enrolled as of the end of the reporting period, both full- and part-time, with each discrete student counted once regardless of their number of courses.

metrics.name: Claims Rejected
metrics.definition: Number of claims submitted to the organization that were rejected during the reporting period.

metrics.name: Payments to Supplier Individuals: Urban
metrics.definition: Value of payments made by the organization to individuals residing in urban areas who sold goods or services to the organization during the reporting period.

metrics.name: Species Recovery
metrics.definition: The annual percentage change in the population size of a target species within a defined area, compared to a scientifically established baseline.

metrics.name: Average Client Agricultural Yield: Total

metrics.name: Product Hours of Light Available

metrics.name: Students Provided Partial Scholarship

metrics.name: Job Placement Rate

metrics.name: Child Stunting Prevalence

metrics.name: Teacher Attendance Rate

metrics.name: Volume of Plastic Removed

metrics.name: Jobs Created at Directly Supported/Financed Enterprises: Total

metrics.name: Service Interruptions

metrics.name: Minimum Insurance Premium

metrics.name: Student Attendance Rate

metrics.name: Area of Trees Planted: Native Species

metrics.name: Healthcare Crude Coverage

metrics.name: Number of Loans Disbursed

metrics.name: Space Per Passenger

metrics.name: Distributor Organizations: Microenterprises

metrics.name: Units/Volume Purchased from Supplier Organizations: Local
metrics.definition: Units/volume from local organizations that sold to the reporting organization during the reporting period.

metrics.name: Units/Volume Sold: No Direct Payment
metrics.definition: Amount of the product/service sold during the reporting period for which no direct payment was provided to the organization at the time of service but for which the organization expects to be reimbursed.

metrics.name: Distributor Individuals: Historically Marginalized

metrics.name: Percent Supplier Payments to Stallholders

metrics.name: Energy Generated for Sale: Total

metrics.name: Client Individuals: New

metrics.name: Client Housing Quality Satisfaction

metrics.name: Number of Insurance Policies

metrics.name: Vocational/Technical Training
metrics.definition: Number of students receiving vocational or technical training during the reporting period.

metrics.name: Promptness of Claims Processing
metrics.definition: Average number of days elapsed between the submission of a completed claim by a stakeholder (client of the organization) and their receipt of payment or claim denial, for all claims settled during the reporting period.

metrics.name: Earnings of Distributor Individuals: Rural
metrics.definition: Earnings of individual distributors residing in rural areas as a result of selling the organization’s products/services during the reporting period.

metrics.name: Payments to Supplier Individuals: Low Income

metrics.name: Average Student Test Score
metrics.definition: Average test score (as a percentage of a perfect score) earned by students served by the organization during the reporting period.

metrics.name: Units/Volume Sold: Exported
metrics.definition: Amount of the product/service exported by the organization during the reporting period.

metrics.name: Payments to Supplier Individuals: Very Poor
metrics.definition: Value of payments made by the organization to very poor individuals who sold goods or services to the organization during the reporting period.

metrics.name: Distributor Individuals: Very Poor
metrics.definition: Number of very poor individuals who served as distributors of the organization’s products/services during the reporting period.

metrics.name: Number of Threatened Species
metrics.definition: Number of threatened species present on land directly controlled by the organization during the reporting period.

metrics.name: Area of Buildings Reused
metrics.definition: Area of buildings projected to be renovated or remodeled for building reuse as a result of investments made by the organization during the reporting period.

metrics.name: Active Borrowers per Loan Officer
metrics.definition: Number of active borrowers (clients) per loan officer at the organization as of the end of the reporting period.

metrics.name: Supplier Individuals: Historically Marginalized
metrics.definition: Number of individuals who belong to groups historically marginalized on the basis of race and/or ethnicity and who sold goods or services to the organization during the reporting period.

metrics.name: Client Retention Rate
metrics.definition: Ratio of the number of the organization's active clients retained from as of the end of the reporting period compared to the number of active clients with which the organization began the reporting period.

metrics.name: Client Individuals: Active
metrics.definition: Number of unique individuals who were active clients of the organization as of the end of the reporting period.

metrics.name: Distributor Organizations: SME
metrics.definition: Number of small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) that served as distributors of the organization’s products/services during the reporting period.

metrics.name: Client Income
metrics.definition: Disaggregates another metric in terms of client income. For example, Client Individuals (PI4060) disaggregated by this metric would yield the number of clients with increased, decreased, or no change in income. See usage guidance for further information. Report all: - Large increase in income (double or more) - Small increase in income (less than doubled) - Decrease in income - No change in income - Data not available - Other (write-in)

metrics.name: Average Client Agriculture Yield: Smallholder
metrics.definition: Average agricultural yield per hectare of clients (who were smallholder farmers) of the organization during the reporting period.

metrics.name: Units/Volume Purchased from Supplier Individuals: Female
metrics.definition: Units/volume purchased from female individuals who sold to the organization during the reporting period.

metrics.name: Client Complaint Tracking System
metrics.definition: Indicates whether the organization has a system in place to receive and resolve client complaints.

metrics.name: Energy Capacity Added
metrics.definition: Amount of new energy capacity connected to the grid during the reporting period, measured in MW.

metrics.name: Job Placements
metrics.definition: Number of the organization's clients who were placed in part-time, full-time, temporary, or permanent jobs during the reporting period.

metrics.name: Gender Ratio of Promotions
metrics.definition: Ratio between the number of female employees promoted from within the organization and the number of non-female employees promoted from within the organization during the reporting period.

metrics.name: Water Provided for Sale: Total
metrics.definition: Volume of water provided and delivered to offtaker(s) during the reporting period.

metrics.definition: Earnings of individuals who belong to groups historically marginalized due to race and/or ethnicity as a result of selling the organization’s products/services during the reporting period.

metrics.definition: Earnings of individual distributors residing in urban areas as a result of selling the organization’s products/services during the reporting period.

metrics.definition: Number of microenterprises that were clients of the organization during the reporting period.

metrics.name: Client Individuals: Very Poor
metrics.definition: Number of unique very poor individuals who were clients of the organization during the reporting period.

metrics.name: Payments to Supplier Individuals: Peri-Urban
metrics.definition: Value of payments made by the organization to individuals residing in peri-urban areas who sold goods or services to the organization during the reporting period.

metrics.name: Client Dropout Reasons
metrics.definition: Describes main reasons clients drop out of a service or program (e.g., time constraints, financial constraints, disinterest).

metrics.name: Greenhouse Gas Emissions Sequestered
metrics.definition: Amount of greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions sequestered by the organization during the reporting period.

metrics.name: Promptness of Claims Settlement
metrics.definition: Average number of days elapsed between the occurrence of an insured incident and the receipt of payment or claim denial by the stakeholder (client of the organization), for all claims settled (or rejected) during the reporting period.

metrics.name: Student Dropout Rate
metrics.definition: Percentage of students who were attending school at the beginning of the reporting period but who dropped out during the reporting period.

metrics.name: Earnings of Distributor Individuals: Peri-urban
metrics.definition: Value of payments made by the organization to individuals residing in peri-urban areas who sold goods or services to the organization during the reporting period.

metrics.name: Purchase Contracts
metrics.definition: Number of contracts and purchase agreements that the organization holds for purchase of its products/services. Disaggregate by contracts fulfilled and contracts outstanding as of the end of the reporting period.

metrics.name: Supplier Individuals: Smallholder
metrics.definition: Number of smallholder farmer individuals who sold to the organization during the reporting period.

metrics.name: Provided New Access
metrics.definition: Describes the type of prior access in a market available to an organization's clients, or disaggregates another metric in terms of type of prior access available in a market. Select any that apply by impact theme: Clean Energy - Previously accessed ‘dirty’ energy - Previously lacked any energy access - Previously accessed less efficient energy - Did not previously lack clean energy access - Other (write-in) Other - Write-in

metrics.name: Product Actual Lifetime
metrics.definition: Average duration a product remains in functional use before being discarded, replaced or retired. This reflects the actual lifespan of the product, based on user behavior, technical durability and serviceability.

metrics.name: Outflow of Recovered Materials
metrics.definition: Total mass of materials that exit the organization’s operations but are diverted from disposal with the aim of reintroducing them into value-retaining cycles.

metrics.name: Recovery Type
metrics.definition: Method by which a product, component or material is recovered at the end of its use phase in order to retain value, extend useful life and reduce waste.

metrics.name: Mass of Products Recovered
metrics.definition: Mass of products or product components recovered through circular strategies, such as reuse, refurbishment, remanufacturing, repair, repurpose or recycling as measured at the point of recovery.

metrics.name: Mass of Products Recovered via Lifetime Extension
metrics.definition: Mass of products or components recovered through lifetime extension strategies.

metrics.name: Input Mass to Recycling Operation
metrics.definition: Mass of material that enters a recycling operation, including both recyclable and non-recyclable materials.

metrics.name: Mass of Input Material Converted into Usable Recycled Output
metrics.definition: Mass of the input material that is successfully transformed into usable recycled material.

metrics.name: Usable Output Mass
metrics.definition: Mass of material output from recycling or processing operations that meets defined quality standards for reuse, resale or reintegration into manufacturing processes.

metrics.name: Access to Personal Protective Equipment (PPE)
metrics.definition: Number of workers with access to appropriate personal protective equipment (PPE) required for their job function within an organization.

metrics.name: Fatal Occupational Injuries
metrics.definition: Number of fatal occupational injuries sustained by members of an organization's workforce.

metrics.name: Number of Workplace Hazards Identified
metrics.definition: Number of workplace hazards identified at the organization’s worksites.

metrics.name: Number of Workers Under Regular Occupational Safety and Health (OSH) Surveillance or Exams
metrics.definition: Number of workers who are enrolled in and receive regular occupational safety and health (OSH) surveillance or medical examinations.

metrics.name: Diverted Material Outflows
metrics.definition: Mass of material that exits the organization’s operational boundary through recovery or disposal pathways, excluding primary products sold.
